govardhan_places.name: Govardhan Hill
govardhan_places.address: Govardhan Hill, Govardhan, Mathura, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: The sacred Govardhan Hill, worshipped as Giriraj Ji — a direct manifestation of Lord Krishna himself. According to the Bhagavata Purana, young Krishna lifted this hill on his little finger for seven days to protect the villagers of Braj from Indra's devastating rains. The hill stretches about 8 km and is circumambulated by millions of devotees performing the 21 km Govardhan Parikrama.
govardhan_places.best_time_to_visit: Early morning or late evening for parikrama; full moon nights are special

govardhan_places.name: Mansi Ganga
govardhan_places.address: Mansi Ganga Kund, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: A sacred lake at the base of Govardhan Hill, believed to have been created by Krishna's mind (manas) to fulfil the gopis' wish to bathe in the Ganges without leaving Braj. The beautiful tank is surrounded by ancient temples and ghats. During Deepawali, thousands of oil lamps float on its waters creating a magical sight.
govardhan_places.best_time_to_visit: Deepawali evening for the lamp festival

govardhan_places.name: Radha Kund
govardhan_places.address: Radha Kund, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: The most sacred bathing place in all of Braj, beloved by Gaudiya Vaishnavas above all other tirthas. According to tradition, Radha created this kund after Krishna killed the Aristotaura (Aristasura) demon. Bathing here on Kartik Purnima is believed to grant devotion equal to Radha's love for Krishna. The twin kunds (Radha Kund and Shyam Kund) lie side by side.

govardhan_places.name: Shyam Kund
govardhan_places.address: Shyam Kund, adjacent to Radha Kund, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: The twin sacred kund of Radha Kund, associated directly with Lord Krishna (Shyam). According to tradition, Krishna created this kund to purify himself after slaying the Aristasura demon. The two kunds are connected underground and their water levels mysteriously rise and fall together. Revered by Vaishnavas as Krishna's personal bathing place.

govardhan_places.name: Danghati Temple
govardhan_places.address: Danghati Temple, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: Situated at the main entrance of Govardhan town, this temple marks the spot where Krishna collected a tax (dan) of butter and curd from the gopis passing through. The temple is the traditional starting point for the Govardhan Parikrama and bustles with activity as pilgrims begin their sacred circumambulation.

govardhan_places.name: Kusum Sarovar
govardhan_places.address: Kusum Sarovar, Govardhan Parikrama Marg, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: One of the most beautiful and historically significant sites on the Govardhan Parikrama route. This magnificent stepped reservoir was rebuilt in the 18th century by the Jat king Suraj Mal. The ornate sandstone pavilions and cenotaphs surrounding the lake are exquisite examples of Rajput-Mughal architecture. It is said that Radha's sakhis would gather flowers (kusum) here for her.
govardhan_places.best_time_to_visit: Sunrise or sunset for best photography

govardhan_places.name: Narad Kund
govardhan_places.address: Narad Kund, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: A sacred water tank associated with the celestial sage Narada, who is said to have performed penance here to receive darshan of Krishna. The kund is believed to be the place where Narada received the divine Narada Bhakti Sutras. Situated along the Parikrama route, it offers a quiet spot for reflection amid the pilgrimage walk.

govardhan_places.name: Govinda Kund
govardhan_places.address: Govinda Kund, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: A large sacred tank where Lord Indra is believed to have performed the Govinda Abhisheka — bathing Krishna with celestial waters and crowning him 'Govinda' (protector of cows) after the Govardhan Leela. The kund is an important stop on the parikrama and features beautiful stone steps descending to the water.

govardhan_places.name: Jatipura Mukharvind
govardhan_places.description: Located at Jatipura village at the western end of Govardhan Hill, this temple houses the Mukharvind — the sacred face (mukh) of Giriraj Ji (Govardhan Hill). Devotees worship this natural rock formation as the face of Krishna himself. The temple is one of the most important stops on the parikrama route.

govardhan_places.name: Punchari Ka Lautha
govardhan_places.address: Punchari Ka Lautha, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: A significant temple on the Govardhan Parikrama route, marking the spot where Madhavendra Puri — the guru of Lord Chaitanya's guru — discovered the Gopal deity inside Govardhan Hill. The deity instructed Madhavendra Puri to build a temple, an event that catalysed the Gaudiya Vaishnava movement. An important pilgrimage site for devotees of the Chaitanya tradition.

govardhan_places.name: Haridev Temple
govardhan_places.address: Haridev Temple, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: One of the oldest and most important temples in Govardhan, established by Shri Vallabhacharya in the early 16th century. The temple is dedicated to Haridev (a form of Krishna) and is one of the original seats of the Pushti Marg tradition. The deity here is believed to have been worshipped since the time of Krishna's grandson Vajranabh.

govardhan_places.name: Rudra Kund
govardhan_places.address: Rudra Kund, near Radha Kund, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: A sacred kund associated with Lord Shiva (Rudra), situated near the Radha Kund–Shyam Kund complex. According to tradition, Lord Shiva performed penance here to witness the divine pastimes of Radha and Krishna. The quiet kund offers a contemplative atmosphere away from the busier pilgrimage spots.

govardhan_places.name: Sankarshan Kund
govardhan_places.address: Sankarshan Kund, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: A sacred water tank named after Sankarshan (Balarama), Krishna's elder brother. The kund lies along the parikrama route between Radha Kund and Govardhan town. Devotees believe bathing here is auspicious and purifying. The peaceful surroundings make it a welcome resting point during the long parikrama walk.

govardhan_places.name: Daan Ghati Market
govardhan_places.address: Daan Ghati, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: The main market area of Govardhan, located near the Danghati Temple at the starting point of the parikrama. The market is bustling with shops selling puja items, Giriraj Ji stones, devotional souvenirs, traditional sweets, and snacks for pilgrims. The atmosphere is vibrant, especially on weekends and festival days.

govardhan_places.name: Radha Kund Bazaar
govardhan_places.address: Radha Kund Bazaar, near Radha Kund, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: A small but significant market near the sacred Radha Kund, serving the needs of pilgrims and resident sadhus. The bazaar offers puja items, simple vegetarian meals, and devotional books. The area has a deeply spiritual atmosphere with many ashrams and bhajan halls in the vicinity.

govardhan_places.name: ISKCON Govardhan
govardhan_places.address: ISKCON Govardhan, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: The ISKCON temple in Govardhan, offering a serene devotional experience near the sacred hill. The temple features beautiful deities, regular kirtan sessions, and clean prasadam facilities. It serves as a base for ISKCON devotees visiting Govardhan and provides comfortable accommodation for pilgrims.

govardhan_places.name: Parikrama Food Stalls
govardhan_places.address: Along Govardhan Parikrama Marg, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: Simple food stalls and rest stops along the 21 km Govardhan Parikrama route, serving chai, lassi, poha, samosas, and other snacks to weary pilgrims. Many stalls offer free water and simple food as seva (service). These humble stops are a lifeline for devotees walking the parikrama barefoot.

govardhan_places.name: Chakleshwar Mahadev
govardhan_places.address: Chakleshwar Mahadev Temple, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: An ancient Shiva temple atop a small rise near the Govardhan Hill, believed to be one of the sacred sites where Lord Shiva stands guard over the Braj region. The Shivalinga here is said to have been established by Krishna's grandson Vajranabh. The temple offers beautiful views of Mansi Ganga and the surrounding landscape.

govardhan_places.name: Govardhan Parikrama Marg
govardhan_places.address: Govardhan Parikrama Marg, Govardhan, Uttar Pradesh, India
govardhan_places.description: The sacred 21 km circumambulation path around Govardhan Hill, one of the most important parikramas in Hinduism. Devotees walk this route barefoot, touching the sacred stones and offering prayers at numerous temples and kunds along the way. The full parikrama takes 5–7 hours on foot. Many perform dandvat parikrama (prostrating their entire body length at every step), which can take weeks to complete.
govardhan_places.best_time_to_visit: Early dawn start; October–March for pleasant weather
